target: FReD / FORRT Replication Database export and R package
reason: Highest-value psychology anchor and dedupe authority; described as pairing many original and replication findings.
what_to_check: Downloadable CSV/R package/export | source-family labels | original effect, replication effect, N, DOI, source family, success classification columns

target: SCORE public outputs and raw claim database
reason: Cross-field high-N claim/repeatability source with multiple public outputs and likely separate replication/reproducibility/robustness components.
what_to_check: Claim sample and repeatability subset deposits | machine-readable outcome tables | links from claims to original papers, reports, effects, and sample sizes

target: I4R website, discussion papers, Replication Games, and Social Science Reproduction Platform
reason: Likely largest current social-science reproduction/replication pipeline; may require scraping plus RePEc/EconStor metadata.
what_to_check: Structured reports endpoint | original article DOI links | replication package and robustness fields | direct replications versus same-data reproductions

target: Brazilian Reproducibility Initiative OSF, GitHub, and Dataverse/NLM record
reason: Rare non-psychology multicenter biomedical replication corpus with public data traces and already integrated local outputs.
what_to_check: Master experiment table | lab IDs | validity flags | effect estimates and sample sizes | whether original identifiers are public/blinded

reason: High-N biology claim-level conceptual replication database, possibly behind web/ORCID boundaries.
what_to_check: Downloadable article/claim tables | numeric/categorical reproducibility outcomes | original metadata and independent-source evidence

reason: Healthcare database reproducibility project with reported reproduction of many studies, but public structured data is not obvious.
what_to_check: Project outputs, supplements, ENCePP registration, data/code availability | original and reproduced measures | reproducibility-only versus robustness/conceptual replication

target: OpenReview MLRC and NeurIPS/ICLR reproducibility challenge venue groups
reason: High-volume ML replication-report source best handled through venue/group API extraction.
what_to_check: Group IDs and invitations | original-paper metadata | comparable original versus reproduced metrics

target: ReScience C contents and BibTeX/data source
reason: Static source exposes structured DOI/code/review metadata for computational replication articles.
what_to_check: Site repository machine-readable BibTeX/YAML/Markdown | mapping from Re titles to originals | data_doi, code_doi, review_url fields

target: NWO/Open Science NL Replication Studies project list
reason: Cross-field funder registry with completed/re-examined studies and fragmented outputs.
what_to_check: Project database filters/award IDs | linked datasets/publications/results | effect/sample information versus qualitative reports

reason: Named large-scale source family outside psychology.
what_to_check: Primary publication supplement and OSF files | target-study list | original effect/sample metadata | aggregate-only versus effect-level data

target: X-Phi Replicability Project OSF and Yale page
reason: Source-family page by original paper with likely OSF project links.
what_to_check: Master original-study and replication list | effect-size and N availability | whether Yale ratings are enough or paper-level extraction needed

target: CORE JDM, Data Replicada, CREP, and Hagen Cumulative Science Project
reason: FORRT-listed large-scale projects that may appear as OSF projects, blogs, registries, or FReD components.
what_to_check: Canonical project pages | multi-row inventories | overlap with FReD | extractable effects in individual projects

target: Industrial Bayer and Amgen preclinical validation corpora
reason: Historically influential but likely non-public at row level.
what_to_check: Supplementary list of original papers | later reviews revealing targets | author/library routes for nonconfidential metadata

target: F1000Research Preclinical Reproducibility and Robustness Gateway
reason: Useful for individual paper worklists and maybe gateway source family, but not standardized.
what_to_check: Gateway article list and tags | target original DOI and result classification | export/API endpoint

target: GWAS Catalog and genetics replication-cohort conventions
reason: Potentially high-N if discovery/replication cohort fields are separable, but risky for Figure 1 semantics.
what_to_check: API/spreadsheet discovery versus replication samples | effect estimates for both cohorts | within-paper validation versus independent replication
